questions: Analyze the history of the Russian Men's Handball Super League (Championship) since its inception in 1992 to identify two clubs with historically significant milestones. First, identify the club that holds the all-time record for the most championship titles. Specify the total number of titles attributed to this franchise (including its direct predecessor) and the distinct team name it used during its victories in the 1999/00 and 2000/01 seasons before adopting its current 'ursine' branding. Second, identify the club that won the league's very first championship in the 1992/93 season. Trace this franchise's history to determine the name under which it competes today—a name shared with a prominent Saint Petersburg football club. Describe the historical rebranding evolution that connects the 1992/93 winner to this modern entity.

logic_chain: Чемпионат России по гандболу среди мужчин — Википедия
rounds.question: Analyze the history of the Russian Men's Handball Super League (Championship) since its inception in 1992 to identify two clubs with historically significant milestones. First, identify the club that holds the all-time record for the most championship titles. Specify the total number of titles attributed to this franchise (including its direct predecessor) and the distinct team name it used during its victories in the 1999/00 and 2000/01 seasons before adopting its current 'ursine' branding. Second, identify the club that won the league's very first championship in the 1992/93 season. Trace this franchise's history to determine the name under which it competes today—a name shared with a prominent Saint Petersburg football club. Describe the historical rebranding evolution that connects the 1992/93 winner to this modern entity.
checklist_width: Record Holder Name: Chekhovskie Medvedi (or Chekhov Bears)
Record Holder Predecessor: CSKA-Sportakadem (must mention this specific name for the 1999-2001 period)
Record Holder Title Count: 23 (or 21 as Bears + 2 as Sportakadem)
Inaugural Winner (1992/93): Neva (or Stepan Razin-Neva / Universitet Lesgafta-Neva)
Inaugural Winner Current Name: Zenit (Saint Petersburg)
Rebranding Details: Mention the transition from Neva/Lesgafta to Zenit
checklist_depth: Logic Proof 1: Correctly links the 1999/00 & 2000/01 titles to Chekhovskie Medvedi via the CSKA-Sportakadem rename history.
Logic Proof 2: Correctly identifies the 1992/93 champion (Neva) and links it to the modern 'Zenit' entity despite the name changes.
rounds.rationale: The question requires Deep Reasoning to connect different historical names (CSKA-Sportakadem -> Chekhovskie Medvedi and Neva -> Zenit) to single entities based on specific season victories (1999-01 and 1992/93). It requires Wide Aggregation to gather the total title counts and the specific evolution of names over a 30-year period, details which are often scattered across different sections of historical records.
source_nodes: Чемпионат России по гандболу среди мужчин — Википедия
rounds.meta_context_snippet: [Wide Logic - Target 0]: Чемпионат России по гандболу среди мужчин Материал из Википедии — свободной энциклопедии Перейти к навигации Перейти к поиску Чемпионат России по гандболу Основан 1992 Регион

rounds.answer_b:
The club with the most championship titles is Chekhovskiye Medvedi (Chekhov Bears), with a total of 23 Russian championships as of 2024 [1]. This total includes the legacy of its direct predecessor, MAI Moscow. The team won the 1999/00 and 2000/01 titles under the distinct name "Luch Moscow" before adopting its current ursine (bear) identity and relocating to Chekhov [1][2].

The winner of the very first championship in the 1992/93 season was the club "Kunst & Jugend" from Saint Petersburg [3][4]. This franchise underwent a series of rebrandings and mergers. It later became known as "Neva," then merged with another club to form "Neva-St. Petersburg." In 2008, it was renamed "Saint Petersburg HC," and in 2011, it adopted its current name, "HC Zenit-Saint Petersburg" [3][4]. This final name is shared with the prominent Saint Petersburg football club, FC Zenit. The handball club's evolution connects the original 1992/93 champion to the modern Zenit brand, which is owned by Gazprom and represents a major multi-sport entity in the city.

## References
[1] Wikipedia - Chekhovskiye Medvedi. Retrieved from https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Chekhovskiye_Medvedi
[2] Wikipedia - Russian Men's Handball Super League. Retrieved from https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Russian_Men%27s_Handball_Super_League
[3] HC Zenit-Saint Petersburg Official History. Retrieved from https://zenit-handball.ru/en/history/
[4] Wikipedia - HC Zenit-Saint Petersburg. Retrieved from https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/HC_Zenit-Saint_Petersburg
